trans-unit.source: Spatial Joins
trans-unit.target: Spatial Joins

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: Join Attributes by location
trans-unit.target: Join Attributes by location

trans-unit.source: Adds additional attributes of the join layer to the input layer based on the __spatial relationship__.
trans-unit.target: Adds additional attributes of the join layer to the input layer based on the __spatial relationship__.

trans-unit.source: `Input Layer`: dataset you want to enrich
trans-unit.target: `Input Layer`: dataset you want to enrich

trans-unit.source: `Join layer`: dataset with additional information/attributes
trans-unit.target: `Join layer`: dataset with additional information/attributes

trans-unit.source: You can specify which fields of the join layer should be added
trans-unit.target: You can specify which fields of the join layer should be added

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Add the time zone (join layer) to the cities (input layer) :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Add the time zone (join layer) to the cities (input layer) :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Join Attributes by location (summary)
trans-unit.target: Join Attributes by location (summary)

trans-unit.source: Adds additional attributes of the join layer to the input layer based on the __spatial relationship__.
trans-unit.target: Adds additional attributes of the join layer to the input layer based on the __spatial relationship__.

trans-unit.source: `Input Layer`: dataset you want to enrich
trans-unit.target: `Input Layer`: dataset you want to enrich

trans-unit.source: `Join layer`: dataset with additional information/attributes
trans-unit.target: `Join layer`: dataset with additional information/attributes

trans-unit.source: Additionally calculates statistical summaries for the values from matching features in the second layer
trans-unit.target: Additionally calculates statistical summaries for the values from matching features in the second layer

trans-unit.source: Options: min, max, mean, count, sum
trans-unit.target: Options: min, max, mean, count, sum

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Solution: Calculate sum of affected population and flooded area for the Area of interest :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Solution: Calculate sum of affected population and flooded area for the Area of interest :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Spatial joins relationships
trans-unit.target: Spatial joins relationships

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Intersect Tests whether the geometry of the two layers intersects with one another. Returns 1 (true) if the geometries spatially intersect (share any portion of space, could overlap or touch) and 0 if they don’t. In the picture above, this will return circles 1, 2 and 3. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Intersect Tests whether the geometry of the two layers intersects with one another. Returns 1 (true) if the geometries spatially intersect (share any portion of space, could overlap or touch) and 0 if they don’t. In the picture above, this will return circles 1, 2 and 3. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Contain Returns 1 (true) if and only if no points of b lie in the exterior of a, and at least one point of the interior of b lies in the interior of a. In the picture, no circle is returned, but the rectangle would be if you would look for it the other way around, as it contains circle 1 completely. This is the opposite of are within. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Contain Returns 1 (true) if and only if no points of b lie in the exterior of a, and at least one point of the interior of b lies in the interior of a. In the picture, no circle is returned, but the rectangle would be if you would look for it the other way around, as it contains circle 1 completely. This is the opposite of are within. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Disjoint Returns 1 (true) if the geometries do not share any portion of space (no overlap, not touching). Only circle 4 is returned. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Disjoint Returns 1 (true) if the geometries do not share any portion of space (no overlap, not touching). Only circle 4 is returned. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Equal Returns 1 (true) if the geometries are exactly the same. No circles will be returned. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Equal Returns 1 (true) if the geometries are exactly the same. No circles will be returned. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Touch Tests whether a geometry touches another. Returns 1 (true) if the geometries have at least one point in common, but their interiors do not intersect. Only circle 3 is returned. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Touch Tests whether a geometry touches another. Returns 1 (true) if the geometries have at least one point in common, but their interiors do not intersect. Only circle 3 is returned. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Overlap Tests whether geometries overlap. Returns 1 (true) if the geometries share space, are of the same dimension, but are not completely contained by each other. Only circle 2 is returned. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Overlap Tests whether geometries overlap. Returns 1 (true) if the geometries share space, are of the same dimension, but are not completely contained by each other. Only circle 2 is returned. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Are within Tests whether one geometry is within another. Returns 1 (true) if geometry a is completely inside geometry b. Only circle 1 is returned. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Are within Tests whether one geometry is within another. Returns 1 (true) if geometry a is completely inside geometry b. Only circle 1 is returned. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Cross Returns 1 (true) if the supplied geometries have some, but not all, interior points in common and the actual crossing is of a lower dimension than the highest supplied geometry. For example, a line crossing a polygon will cross as a line (true). Two lines crossing will cross as a point (true). Two polygons cross as a polygon (false). In the picture, no circles will be returned. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Cross Returns 1 (true) if the supplied geometries have some, but not all, interior points in common and the actual crossing is of a lower dimension than the highest supplied geometry. For example, a line crossing a polygon will cross as a line (true). Two lines crossing will cross as a point (true). Two polygons cross as a polygon (false). In the picture, no circles will be returned. :::
